message.content: Adversaries may exploit improperly secured cloud storage to access sensitive data. Cloud storage solutions like Amazon S3, Azure Storage, and Google Cloud Storage, as well as SaaS applications such as Slack and Salesforce, can be directly accessed via APIs. Common vulnerabilities include misconfigurations that allow public or overly-broad access, exposing data such as credit card information, personally identifiable information, or medical records. Adversaries may also use leaked credentials from source repositories or logs to gain unauthorized access. Notable examples include Fox Kitten accessing victim cloud storage and Peirates dumping AWS S3 bucket contents. Mitigation strategies include following cloud provider security guides, implementing strict Identity Access Management (IAM) policies, regularly auditing access permissions, and monitoring for unusual access patterns to detect and respond to potential breaches.
